# Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

We as members, contributors, and leaders of the Aarya — My AI Learning Hub community pledge to make participation in our project a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, body size, visible or invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity and expression, level of experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, or sexual identity and orientation.

We pledge to act and interact in ways that contribute to an open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, and healthy community.

## Our Standards

**Examples of behavior that contributes to a positive environment:**

- Using welcoming and inclusive language
- Being respectful of differing viewpoints and experience levels
- Giving and gracefully accepting constructive feedback
- Focusing on what is best for the community of learners
- Showing empathy and patience — many contributors are actively studying and learning

**Examples of unacceptable behavior:**

- Sexualised language or imagery, or unwelcome advances
- Trolling, insulting, or derogatory comments; personal or political attacks
- Public or private harassment
- Publishing others' private information without explicit permission
- Submitting plagiarised exam content or copyrighted material without attribution
- Other conduct that could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional setting

## Enforcement Responsibilities

Project maintainers are responsible for clarifying and enforcing standards of acceptable behavior and will take appropriate and fair corrective action in response to any behavior they deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, or harmful.

Maintainers have the right to remove, edit, or reject comments, commits, code, issues, and other contributions that do not align with this Code of Conduct, and will communicate reasons for moderation decisions when appropriate.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies within all project spaces — GitHub Issues, PRs, Discussions, the Contribute form on the platform, and any other official channels. It also applies when an individual is representing the project in public spaces.

## Enforcement

Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be reported by:

1. Using the [GitHub Security Advisory](https://github.com/ajeetchouksey/ajch_platform/security/advisories/new) for private reports, OR
2. Contacting the maintainer directly via the GitHub profile

All complaints will be reviewed promptly. The maintainer is obligated to maintain confidentiality of the reporter.

## Enforcement Guidelines

| Violation | Consequence |
|-----------|-------------|
| Minor / first offence — inappropriate language, off-topic | Written warning; content removed |
| Repeated or targeted behaviour | Temporary block from contributions |
| Sustained harassment or serious violation | Permanent ban from the project |

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/), version 2.1, available at https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html.
